serving.natural_language_portion: One whole pizza of approximately 350 g, typical of a restaurant-style individual Italian pizza served at Mingle, Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat, NS as to type of crust (SR Legacy 21299) and component ingredient data for chorizo, mozzarella, olives, and hard cheese
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Iberico chorizo pizza exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composition of USDA FoodData Central entries for: pizza dough/crust (SR Legacy), fior di latte mozzarella, dry-cured chorizo (Spanish-style pork sausage), taggiasche olives, grana padano (hard Italian cheese), and chilli oil. Proportions estimated from standard Italian pizza construction. Energy cross-checked against USDA pizza with meat entries. Values represent a best-estimate per 100 g of finished pizza.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Pizza, plain baked dough
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Iberico chorizo pizza. GI of 50 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for pizza (plain, baked dough, GI ~50, low category), which is the closest analogous food in an approved GI source. The high fat and protein content from chorizo, mozzarella, and grana padano are expected to further attenuate glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 86.8) / 100 = 43.4, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 24.8 × 3.5 = 86.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g; ~2170 mg per whole pizza) from dry-cured chorizo, grana padano, and olives
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this pizza due to its very high sodium load from cured meats, aged cheese, and brined olives, which can significantly elevate blood pressure; a single serving may exceed the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from refined wheat dough, partially offset by high fat and protein content slowing glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this pizza in small portions, as the refined wheat crust contributes a moderate glycemic load (~43 GL per whole pizza); the fat and protein from chorizo and cheese attenuate the glycemic response, but total carbohydrate intake must still be monitored carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from iberico chorizo and aged cheeses, combined with high sodium and cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This pizza is high in saturated fat (~5.1 g per 100 g) and sodium (~620 mg per 100 g) from iberico dry-cured chorizo and grana padano, making it a high-concern food for individuals with cardiovascular disease who should limit intake of cured meats and high-fat dairy products.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iberico Chorizo Pizza (also listed as 'Iberico Chorizo Daviola' on the menu at Mingle, Funan, Singapore) is an Italian-style pizza topped with fior di latte mozzarella, thinly sliced iberico dry-cured chorizo, taggiasche olives, grana padano cheese, and chilli oil. The pizza is baked in a high-temperature oven, producing a thin, slightly charred crust with a soft, chewy interior. The flavour profile is rich and savoury: the iberico chorizo contributes deep, smoky, and slightly spicy pork fat notes; the fior di latte provides a mild, creamy dairy base; the taggiasche olives add a fruity, briny contrast; the grana padano lends a sharp, nutty umami finish; and the chilli oil adds a gentle heat. Nutritionally, this pizza is energy-dense (~268 kcal per 100 g) with moderate protein (~12.5 g), significant fat (~13.2 g, of which ~5.1 g is saturated), and moderate carbohydrate (~24.8 g) from the wheat dough. Sodium is notably high (~620 mg per 100 g) due to the cured meat, aged cheese, and brined olives. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 50 (low category) based on the pizza dough base, with fat and protein from toppings further attenuating glycemic response. Clinically, this pizza is a high-concern food for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium and saturated fat content, and a moderate-concern food for Type 2 Diabetes due to its refined carbohydrate base. It is not suitable for halal, vegetarian, or vegan diets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Iberico Chorizo Pizza?
Is this pizza suitable for diabetics?
How much sodium is in chorizo pizza?
Is iberico chorizo the same as Spanish chorizo?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific restaurant pizza exists in any approved primary nutrient database. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for pizza dough, dry-cured chorizo, mozzarella, olives, and hard cheese. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (plain pizza) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component ingredients are well-characterised in USDA sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used for this estimation. All other -1 fields have been replaced with estimated values where component data was available. The food_group in the input record was listed as 'Grains and staples', which has been corrected to 'Mixed' as this is a composite pizza dish. The cuisine tag 'Italian' is retained and confirmed. The restaurant 'Mingle - Funan' is located in Funan Mall, Singapore. The dish name 'Daviola' appears to be a menu-specific branding name rather than a standard Italian culinary term; the canonical food name has been set to 'Iberico Chorizo Pizza' with 'Iberico Chorizo Daviola' retained as an alias. No multilingual local-language names are required for Italian cuisine under the Veritas v2 multilingual rules. Sodium content is notably high; a full 350 g pizza would deliver approximately 2170 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg. GL per serving = (50 × 86.8) / 100 = 43.4 g, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 86.8 g.
